entries.title: Faramir Is Not Yet Active In Fellowship

context.label: Faramir Is Not Yet Active In Fellowship

content.fact: Faramir exists as Boromir’s brother in Gondor but should not intrude as a Ring-quest participant in Fellowship.
content.injection: Faramir exists as Boromir’s brother in Gondor but should not intrude as a Ring-quest participant in Fellowship. Keep this hidden from uninformed characters until the relevant reveal or direct evidence.
constraints: Keep this bounded to the declared book-continuity Context.
antiLore: Do not replace book-continuity logic with film-only compression unless a film overlay is active.

entries.title: Gandalf Confirms The Ring’s True Nature

content.fact: Gandalf confirms that Frodo's inherited ring is the One Ring, bound to Sauron and unsafe to keep in the Shire.
content.injection: After Gandalf confirms the Ring, Frodo and Gandalf should understand that the ring is the One Ring and that staying in the Shire exposes Frodo and others to danger.
constraints: Before this anchor, Frodo may suspect little or know only that the ring is unusual.
The knowledge should remain tightly held.
antiLore: Do not let the broader Shire learn the Ring's identity.
Do not make Frodo fully informed before Gandalf's confirmation scene.

entries.title: Gildor Warns Without Taking Over The Quest

context.label: Gildor Warns Without Taking Over The Quest

content.fact: Gildor’s company gives warning and shelter, but does not take command of the Ring or solve the journey.
content.injection: Gildor’s Elves may be wise and helpful, but the Hobbits remain responsible for reaching safety.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

content.fact: Frodo’s friends know more about his departure than he expected and have prepared to help.
content.injection: At Crickhollow, Frodo should learn his companions chose risk and secrecy with him.

entries.title: Fatty Bolger Stays As Crickhollow Decoy

content.fact: Fredegar Bolger stays behind at Crickhollow to maintain Frodo’s cover while Frodo, Sam, Merry, and Pippin leave the Shire.
content.injection: During the Shire departure window, use Fredegar as the local decoy who helps preserve the appearance that Frodo is still near Crickhollow.
constraints: This is local Shire secrecy, not a Fellowship role.
Fredegar does not continue with the main quest.
antiLore: Do not put Fatty Bolger on the road to Rivendell or Mordor in book continuity.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

sourceInfo.title: The Lord of the Rings
sourceInfo.notes: Schema-shaped revision-pass card; requires human canon review before reference release. Chapter assignment generated for v7 review; verify during human canon review.

coordinates.label: Flight to Rivendell
Book I: The Ring Sets Out

context.label: After the Hobbit conspiracy is unmasked, before Bree

content.fact: At Crickhollow, Merry, Pippin, Sam, and Fredegar reveal that they know more about Frodo’s danger and departure than Frodo expected, though they still do not know the full truth of the One Ring.
content.injection: After the conspiracy is unmasked, treat the Hobbit companions as active participants in Frodo’s escape, not accidental followers. Still gate the full Ring-lore: their loyalty exceeds their knowledge.
constraints: The conspiracy should increase loyalty and agency for Merry and Pippin.
Fredegar’s role remains local to the Shire deception rather than the wider quest.
antiLore: Do not portray Merry and Pippin as ignorant stowaways after Crickhollow.
Do not give the Hobbits complete Council-level Ring knowledge here.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

entries.title: Bree-folk Distrust Rangers Without Understanding Them

context.label: Bree-folk Distrust Rangers Without Understanding Them

content.fact: Many Bree-folk see Rangers as strange and suspicious without knowing what dangers they prevent.
content.injection: Bree characters should not automatically respect Strider; suspicion of Rangers is normal.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.

entries.title: Gandalf’s Delayed Letter Is A Trust Pivot

context.label: Gandalf’s Delayed Letter Is A Trust Pivot

content.fact: Butterbur’s delayed letter from Gandalf becomes crucial evidence for trusting Strider.
content.injection: Use Gandalf’s letter as concrete trust evidence; Strider should not be trusted just because he is mysterious.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Glorfindel Replaces Film-Arwen At The Ford

context.label: Glorfindel Replaces Film-Arwen At The Ford

content.fact: In book continuity, Glorfindel aids the flight to the Ford; Arwen does not replace him in this rescue.
content.injection: Use Glorfindel for the Ford rescue in book-continuity scenes unless an AU/film deck changes it.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

context.label: Flight to the Ford before Rivendell

content.fact: In book continuity, Glorfindel plays the key rescue role on the final approach to Rivendell during the Flight to the Ford.
content.injection: For book-canon scenes near the Ford, use Glorfindel as the major Elven rescuer and do not silently substitute the film-only Arwen rescue sequence.
constraints: This protects the declared novel continuity.
A film-continuity overlay can override this if explicitly loaded.
antiLore: Do not import the film Flight to the Ford by default.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

content.fact: Inside Moria, the Fellowship discovers that Balin's Dwarven colony has fallen and that the halls are occupied by deadly enemies.
content.injection: Within Moria after the discovery, treat Gimli's hope as broken by evidence that Balin's colony failed and that the Fellowship is deep in hostile ruins.
constraints: This knowledge should not be assumed before the party reaches the evidence in Moria.
The discovery should alter mood and urgency.
antiLore: Do not let characters outside relevant knowledge already know every detail of Balin's fate.
Do not make Moria feel safe after the discovery.
